description_long: This is a classic biplane with a striking blue and yellow color scheme, featuring bold radial stripes on the wings and tail surfaces. The aircraft has two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by struts and wires, giving it a vintage appearance reminiscent of early 20th-century stunt or trainer planes. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers towards the tail, with a single open cockpit located just behind the upper wing. The nose houses a wooden propeller and a visible engine cowling, while the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, typical of taildragger configurations. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all adorned with matching blue and yellow striping. The overall design suggests a lightweight, maneuverable airplane suitable for aerobatics or display flights in urban parks or airshows.
description: A vintage blue and yellow biplane with bold striped wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and classic taildragger landing gear. Its design is lightweight and visually striking, ideal for aerobatic or display use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue fuselage, yellow stripes, open cockpit, and wooden propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the dual biplane wings with radial yellow stripes and the full tail section.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same fuselage and wing features, with the tailwheel and cockpit visible.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the propeller, landing gear, and the stacked biplane wings with their connecting struts.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets; slight dihedral on lower wing; classic strut-and-wire bracing.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a small fixed tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage.
